definition: A sequence of instructions and rules used by models to learn from data.
A computational model inspired by the human brain structure, consisting of interconnected nodes.
An algorithm used in training neural networks to adjust weights by calculating gradients.
A systematic error in a machine learning model that causes it to produce skewed results.
A type of supervised learning task where the output is a categorical label.
An unsupervised learning task that groups similar data points together.
A field of AI enabling computers to interpret and make decisions based on visual data.
A class of deep neural networks commonly applied to analyzing visual imagery.
The practice of analyzing large databases in order to generate new information.
A subset of machine learning based on artificial neural networks with multiple layers.
The process of reducing the number of random variables under consideration.
One complete pass of the training dataset through the machine learning algorithm.
The process of transforming raw data into numerical features that can be processed while preserving the information.
An optimization algorithm used to minimize the loss function by iteratively moving in the direction of steepest descent.
A parameter whose value is set before the learning process begins.
The process of identifying raw data and adding one or more meaningful and informative labels to provide context.
A method of evaluating how well your algorithm models your dataset.
A branch of AI that helps computers understand, interpret and manipulate human language.
A modeling error that occurs when a function is too closely fit to a limited set of data points.
A configuration variable that is internal to the model and whose value can be estimated from data.
The use of statistics to predict outcomes based on historical data.
An area of ML concerned with how intelligent agents ought to take actions in an environment to maximize cumulative reward.
A machine learning paradigm where the model is trained on explicitly labeled data.
A supervised machine learning algorithm used for both classification and regression challenges.
The initial set of data used to help a machine learning program understand how to apply technologies.
A research problem in machine learning that focuses on storing knowledge gained while solving one problem and applying it to a different but related problem.
Occurs when a model is too simple to capture the underlying structure of the data.
A type of machine learning that looks for previously undetected patterns in a data set with no pre-existing labels.
An error from sensitivity to small fluctuations in the training set.
